sanskrit: अव्यक्ताद्व्यक्तयः सर्वाः प्रभवन्त्यहरागमे । रात्र्यागमे प्रलीयन्ते तत्रैवाव्यक्तसंज्ञके ॥
hindiTranslation: ब्रह्माजीके दिनके आरम्भकालमें अव्यक्त ब्रह्माजीके सूक्ष्मशरीर से सम्पूर्ण प्राणी पैदा होते हैं और ब्रह्माजीकी रातके आरम्भकालमें उसी अव्यक्तमें सम्पूर्ण प्राणी लीन हो जाते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: At the start of Brahma's day, all beings arise from the unmanifest; at the start of Brahma's night, they dissolve back into that same unmanifest.
essence: Everything formed returns to the unmanifest in time.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: All beings emerge from the unmanifest at the beginning of Brahma's day and return to it at the beginning of Brahma's night.
simpleInsight: What appears solid is temporary. Life keeps rising and dissolving in a larger rhythm.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Krishna is answering Arjuna's fear of endings with a cosmic pattern. He explains that beings rise from the unmanifest when Brahma's day begins and return there when night begins, showing that birth and dissolution are part of a vast cycle.
modernRelevance: You watch a relationship, a job, or even your own plans fall apart and feel like everything is ending. This verse zooms out: forms come, forms go, and the deeper pattern continues.
translationLiteral: अव्यक्तात् (from the unmanifest) / व्यक्तयः (manifest beings) / सर्वाः (all) / प्रभवन्ति (arise) / अहरागमे (at the coming of day) / रात्र्यागमे (at the coming of night) / प्रलीयन्ते (dissolve) / तत्रैव (there alone) / अव्यक्तसंज्ञके (in the one called unmanifest)
